title: scroll-like project threads. The “codex architecture” implies a system where all
content: scroll-like project threads. The “codex architecture” implies a system where all content can reside in one navigable container, possibly with sections or chapters but ultimately part of one book of life. The media ontology of such a codex would need to accommodate different content types (text, code, images, etc.) in a unified way. This relates to media ontology efforts in information science that attempt to create a single taxonomy or ontology for all media types and topics, enabling convergence of classification. For instance, library science’s Universal Decimal Classification or modern ontologies (schema.org) aim to provide a common naming for concepts across media (so a photo, article, and video about “Apollo 11” all tie to the same concept node in an ontology). • Knowledge Graphs and Ontologies: In conceptual terms, flattening parallel threads often means finding the underlying graph structure that connects them. Personal knowledge bases implemented as knowledge graphs (nodes and relationships) naturally merge multiple organizational schemes – you might have one hierarchy by topic, another by chronology, but in a graph, those can coexist as different relationship types betwee
context: From document: Transforming a Fragmented Digital Life into a Unified Canonical Architecture — chunk 14 (chunk 14/37)
